task_completion.description: Open and read the work order text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of 'work_order_001.txt' with the sheet metal specifications.

task_completion.description: Create the required CAD layers with correct colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel in LibreCAD. It must show 'CUT_EXTERIOR' (White/Black), 'BEND_LINES' (Yellow), and 'CUT_HOLES' (Red).

task_completion.description: Draw the exterior outline of the flat pattern
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the LibreCAD drawing area for the outer boundary of the flat pattern. It should look like a cross shape (a large rectangle with square cutouts at all four corners) drawn on the CUT_EXTERIOR layer.

task_completion.description: Draw the bend lines separating the base and flanges
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for dashed yellow lines (on the BEND_LINES layer) connecting the inner corners of the cross shape, forming a central rectangle.
Look for exactly 4 red circles (on the CUT_HOLES layer) positioned inside the central rectangle area, near each of the four corners.

task_completion.description: Save the drawing as the specified DXF file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the LibreCAD window title bar or a 'Save As' dialog box to confirm the file is being saved as 'chassis_flat_pattern.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical interface to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent is actively clicking tools, drawing lines/circles, and managing layers within the LibreCAD GUI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal commands running Python scripts or other tools to programmatically generate the DXF file.
Verify across screenshots that the drawing is built step-by-step (e.g., layers created, then outline, then holes), rather than a completed file suddenly appearing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The flat pattern is a cross-like shape with a total bounding box of 170mm x 130mm. The central base is 120mm x 80mm, defined by dashed yellow bend lines (BEND_LINES layer). The exterior outline should be white/black (CUT_EXTERIOR layer). There are exactly 4 red circles (CUT_HOLES layer) representing 6mm diameter holes, located inside the base area, spaced 90mm apart horizontally and 50mm vertically.
